inputs.command:
set -e; TMP=$(mktemp -d -t gad-boot-XXXXXX)

echo "=== CASE 1: fresh onboard ==="
T1=$TMP/fresh
node bin/install.js --claude --new-project "$T1" >/dev/null 2>&1
echo "CLAUDE.md exists: $([ -f $T1/CLAUDE.md ] && echo YES || echo NO)"
echo "AGENTS.md exists: $([ -f $T1/AGENTS.md ] && echo YES || echo NO)"
grep -c "GAD:boot-start" $T1/CLAUDE.md
head -3 $T1/CLAUDE.md

echo; echo "=== CASE 2: re-run (append replaces bounded block) ==="
# Add custom user content above/below the GAD markers
cat > $T1/CLAUDE.md.new <<'EOF'
# Custom project

This is the user's custom header.

<!-- GAD:boot-start -->
OLD STALE GAD CONTENT THAT SHOULD BE REPLACED
<!-- GAD:boot-end -->

## User footer content

Keep this too.
EOF
mv $T1/CLAUDE.md.new $T1/CLAUDE.md
node bin/install.js --claude --new-project "$T1" >/dev/null 2>&1
echo "--- CLAUDE.md after re-run ---"
cat $T1/CLAUDE.md

echo; echo "=== CASE 3: existing file without markers (append prepends) ==="
T3=$TMP/no-markers
mkdir -p $T3
echo "# Existing user CLAUDE.md with no GAD markers" > $T3/CLAUDE.md
echo "Custom content here." >> $T3/CLAUDE.md
node bin/install.js --claude --new-project "$T3" >/dev/null 2>&1
echo "--- first + last 5 lines of CLAUDE.md ---"
head -5 $T3/CLAUDE.md
echo "..."
tail -5 $T3/CLAUDE.md

rm -rf $TMP

=== CASE 2: re-run (append replaces bounded block) ===
--- CLAUDE.md after re-run ---
# Custom project

This is the user's custom header.

<!-- GAD:boot-start -->
## GAD boot contract

**First action in every session:** run the GAD snapshot to hydrate
context from `.planning/`. Do not read planning files manually — the
snapshot gives you STATE, ROADMAP, tasks, decisions, and skills in one
low-token command.

```sh
gad snapshot --projectid fresh
```

### The loop

1. `gad snapshot --projectid fresh` — know where you are
2. Pick one planned task from `.planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml`
3. Implement it
4. Update `TASK-REGISTRY.xml` (status=done, skill, agent, type),
   `STATE.xml` (next-action), `DECISIONS.xml` if anything new
5. Commit

### Planning files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.planning/STATE.xml` | Current phase, milestone, next-action |
| `.planning/ROADMAP.xml` | Phase breakdown with goals + dependencies |
| `.planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml` | All tasks with status, attribution |
| `.planning/DECISIONS.xml` | Architectural decisions |
| `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.xml` | Intent and non-negotiables |

### Context exhaustion

Auto-compact handles it. After compaction, re-run `gad snapshot` to
re-hydrate and continue. Never stop work mid-task.

This block was generated by `bin/install.js --new-project` on
2026-04-15. Re-running the installer with
`--append` will replace only the content between the GAD markers and
leave everything else in this file untouched.
<!-- GAD:boot-end -->


## User footer content

Keep this too.

=== CASE 3: existing file without markers (append prepends) ===
--- first + last 5 lines of CLAUDE.md ---
<!-- GAD:boot-start -->
## GAD boot contract

**First action in every session:** run the GAD snapshot to hydrate
context from `.planning/`. Do not read planning files manually — the
...

# graphify

[![CI](https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg?branch=v1)](https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify/actions/workflows/ci.yml)

**A Claude Code skill.** Type `/graphify` in Claude Code - it reads your files, builds a knowledge graph, and gives you back structure you didn't know was there.

Fully multimodal. Drop in code, PDFs, markdown, screenshots, diagrams, whiteboard photos, even images in other languages - graphify uses Claude vision to extract concepts and relationships from all of it and connects them into one graph.

> Andrej Karpathy keeps a `/raw` folder where he drops papers, tweets, screenshots, and notes. graphify is the answer to that problem - 71.5x fewer tokens per query vs reading the raw files, persistent across sessions, honest about what it found vs guessed.

```
/graphify .                        # works on any folder - your codebase, notes, papers, anything
```

```
graphify-out/
├── graph.html       interactive graph - click nodes, search, filter by community
├── obsidian/        open as Obsidian vault
├── wiki/            Wikipedia-style articles for agent navigation (--wiki)
├── GRAPH_REPORT.md  god nodes, surprising connections, suggested questions
├── graph.json       persistent graph - query weeks later without re-reading
└── cache/           SHA256 cache - re-runs only process changed files
```

## Install

**Requires:** [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) and Python 3.10+

```bash
pip install graphifyy && graphify install
```

> The PyPI package is temporarily named `graphifyy` while the `graphify` name is being reclaimed. The CLI and skill command are still `graphify`.

Then open Claude Code in any directory and type:

```
/graphify .
```

<details>
<summary>Manual install (curl)</summary>

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/graphify
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/safishamsi/graphify/v1/skills/graphify/skill.md \
  > ~/.claude/skills/graphify/SKILL.md
```

Add to `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`:

```
- **graphify** (`~/.claude/skills/graphify/SKILL.md`) - any input to knowledge graph. Trigger: `/graphify`
When the user types `/graphify`, invoke the Skill tool with `skill: "graphify"` before doing anything else.
```

</details>

## Usage

```
/graphify                          # run on current directory
/graphify ./raw                    # run on a specific folder
/graphify ./raw --mode deep        # more aggressive INFERRED edge extraction
/graphify ./raw --update           # re-extract only changed files, merge into existing graph

/graphify add https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762        # fetch a paper, save, update graph
/graphify add https://x.com/karpathy/status/...       # fetch a tweet

/graphify query "what connects attention to the optimizer?"
/graphify path "DigestAuth" "Response"
/graphify explain "SwinTransformer"

/graphify ./raw --watch            # auto-sync graph as files change (code: instant, docs: notifies you)
/graphify ./raw --wiki             # build agent-crawlable wiki (index.md + article per community)
/graphify ./raw --svg              # export graph.svg
/graphify ./raw --graphml          # export graph.graphml (Gephi, yEd)
/graphify ./raw --neo4j            # generate cypher.txt for Neo4j
/graphify ./raw --mcp              # start MCP stdio server

inputs.content:
# Temporary skill install flow + Graphify integration — design

Date: 2026-04-15
Context: operator flagged two related concepts during the onboard flow
pass:
(1) Need a short-lived "try-before-install" mode for skills that produce
    a one-time artifact — codebase map, game scaffold, knowledge graph —
    so users can experiment without committing to a full install.
(2) Want to absorb a Graphify-equivalent knowledge graph + visualization
    into GAD, mirroring what `get-shit-done` did in commit c11ec05, but
    wired into our workflow/site/visual-context-system instead of as a
    bolt-on.

These are independent features but they compose: the temp-skill flow is
the cleanest way for a user to *try* graphify inside a GAD project
without paying the full install cost up front.

## Part 1 — Temporary skill install flow (`gad try <skill>`)

### Problem

A user wants to run one skill (e.g. codebase-map, graphify, game
scaffolder) to produce an artifact, then stop caring about the skill.
Full install via `bin/install.js --new-project <path>` is overkill:
landing 91 skills + .planning/ + hooks just to use one. The user has to
manually clean up afterward, and if the skill installs Python/Node
dependencies or binaries they have no idea what they consented to.

### Proposed shape

New `gad try` subcommand that wraps an ephemeral runtime. Shape:

```sh
gad try <skill-slug-or-url> [-- skill-args...]
# Examples:
gad try graphify -- . --mode deep
gad try codebase-map -- src/
gad try https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify -- . --wiki
```

### Flow

1. **Locate the skill source.**
   - Local slug → `skills/<slug>/SKILL.md` in the framework
   - Installed slug → `~/.claude/skills/<slug>/SKILL.md` (global) or
     `<cwd>/.claude/skills/<slug>/SKILL.md` (local)
   - URL → git clone shallow into a tmp dir, look for `skills/<name>/`
     or a `SKILL.md` at the root
2. **Dependency audit.** Parse SKILL.md frontmatter + body for dependency
   declarations:
   - `requires:` frontmatter key listing binaries (`python3.10+`,

... [truncated 9675 bytes] ...
e already have as the
structural backbone; write extraction rules against the planning XML
schema we own; output the same `graph.json` shape Graphify uses so
third-party tools (Obsidian, Neo4j, Gephi) still work.
Pro: no Python dep, stays entirely inside the Node+JS framework, can
evolve with our planning schema, and the graph can embed decisions and
skills with typed edges from day one. Con: reinvents Graphify's
extraction logic for non-XML files (Markdown, images, PDFs).

**Recommendation: start with Option A via `gad try graphify`** to see
the output shape against a real GAD project, then decide whether to
commit to a vendored copy (A) or build a native extractor (B). The
temporary-skill-install flow from Part 1 is the cheapest way to run
that experiment — one command, no commitment, easy cleanup.

### Proposed sequencing

1. Ship `gad try` (Part 1) as a new task, small CLI surface, no
   external deps.
2. Run `gad try graphify -- .planning skills decisions` against this
   repo, capture the output, eyeball the graph shape.
3. Decide A vs B based on what the output actually contains.
4. Either way: add `site/data/planning-graph.json` to the site data
   pipeline and wire command+K + VCS + DB viewer to read it.
5. Follow up: agent wiki routes if the use case materializes.

## Tasks this note spawns

| Id | Title | Status | Scope |
|---|---|---|---|
| 42.2-39 | CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md writer + hooks fallback | done | shipped same session |
| 42.2-40 | Temporary skill install flow (`gad try`) | planned | new CLI subcommand, consent gate, sandbox, cleanup |
| 42.2-41 | Graphify trial via `gad try` against this repo | planned | experiment, produces a FINDINGS-*.md |
| 42.2-42 | Decision: vendor Graphify vs native extractor | planned | decision doc based on 42.2-41 findings |
| 42.2-43 | `site/data/planning-graph.json` pipeline + command+K integration | planned | depends on 42.2-42 outcome |
| 42.2-44 | VCS `<VisualContextGraphPanel>` primitive | planned | depends on 42.2-43 |

Ships incremental. No single mega-task.

    <task id="42.2-39" type="framework" status="done" skill="default" agent="default"><goal>Fill two gaps in the onboard flow surfaced by operator inspection: (a) no CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md is written at the project root so a fresh Claude Code / Codex session has no boot contract telling it to run `gad startup` first; (b) `bin/install.js` hook install path silently no-ops in a local checkout because it looks for `sdk/hooks/dist` which does not exist in a clone, leaving settings.json wired to .js hook paths that do not resolve. Fix both and add append/overwrite/skip handling so re-runs against existing CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md files don't clobber user content.</goal>
      <resolution>Shipped 2026-04-15. Three changes in one commit:
        (1) `bin/install.js` hooks fallback — prefer `sdk/hooks/dist`, fall back to repo-root `hooks/dist` for js files (mirrors 42.2-35/42.2-36 pattern), plus an idempotent copy from repo-root `hooks/*.sh` for the three shell hooks (gad-phase-boundary, gad-session-state, gad-validate-commit) which live outside the dist tree. Post-fix a fresh onboard lands all 8 hook files at `<folder>/.claude/hooks/` and every settings.json hook reference resolves on disk.
        (2) Boot contract writer — new `writeBootContracts(targetFolder, projectId, projectName, mode)` + helpers `renderGadBootBlock`, `renderBootContractDoc`, `writeBootContractFile`, `detectExistingBootContracts` in bin/install.js. Writes both `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md` at the project root with a bounded `<!-- GAD:boot-start -->` / `<!-- GAD:boot-end -->` block containing the `gad snapshot --projectid <id>` boot instruction, the 5-step loop, the planning-files table, and the context-exhaustion rule.           - `append` (default, safe) — if GAD markers exist, replace only the bounded block; if no markers, prepend a new block at the top with `---` separator, preserv

Blocks 42.2-42.</goal>
      <keywords>eval,graphify,knowledge-graph,trial,findings</keywords>

    </task>

inputs.old_string: Operator can now double-click the installer, pick a folder, and have a working GAD project.
inputs.new_string: Operator can now double-click the installer, pick a folder, and have a working GAD project. 42.2-39 follow-up shipped same session: `bin/install.js` gains a hooks fallback (prefers `sdk/hooks/dist`, falls back to repo-root `hooks/dist` + `hooks/*.sh` in a clone) so all 8 hook files land at `.claude/hooks/` and every settings.json hook reference now resolves; the onboard flow also writes `CLAUDE.md` + `AGENTS.md` at the project root with a bounded `<!-- GAD:boot-start -->` / `<!-- GAD:boot-end -->` block so re-runs against existing files replace only the GAD section and preserve user content verbatim (append default, overwrite + skip available via interactive prompt or `GAD_ONBOARD_BOOT_MODE` env). 20/20 tests green including new Flow D cases covering the bounded-block replacement and hook file presence. Next direction: temporary skill install flow (`gad try <skill>`) designed in `.planning/notes/temp-skill-install-and-graphify-2026-04-15.md` as the enabling feature for trialing Graphify (knowledge graph + visualization) before committing to a vendor-vs-native decision. git add bin/install.js tests/consumer-init-install.test.cjs .planning/notes/temp-skill-install-and-graphify-2026-04-15.md .planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml .planning/STATE.xml && git status --short | grep -E "^[AM]" | head -10 && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
42.2-39: CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md boot contracts + hooks fallback for local checkouts

Operator inspection of the onboard result surfaced two gaps:

1. No CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md written at the project root — a freshly
   onboarded project had 91 installed skills but zero boot contract
   telling Claude Code / Codex / Cursor to run `gad startup` first.
2. bin/install.js hook install silently no-ops in a local checkout —
   looks for sdk/hooks/dist which only exists in packaged release
   builds, so clones leave .claude/settings.json wired to .js hook
   paths that do not resolve on disk.

Fixes shipped together since they both affect the double-click onboard
path.

HOOKS FALLBACK (mirrors 42.2-35/36 pattern)
- Prefer sdk/hooks/dist (release)
- Fall back to repo-root hooks/dist for .js hooks (clone)
- Also copy repo-root hooks/*.sh idempotently for the three shell
  hooks (gad-phase-boundary, gad-session-state, gad-validate-commit)
  that live outside the dist tree
- Post-fix: all 8 hook files land at <folder>/.claude/hooks/ and every
  settings.json reference resolves

CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md WRITER
- New writeBootContracts(targetFolder, projectId, projectName, mode)
  writes both files at the project root with a bounded
  <!-- GAD:boot-start --> / <!-- GAD:boot-end --> block
- Block content: gad snapshot boot command, 5-step loop, planning
  files table, context exhaustion rule
- Three collision modes:
    append    — GAD markers present: replace bounded block only
                no markers: prepend GAD block above --- separator,
                preserve existing content below
    overwrite — replace entire file
    skip      — leave file alone
- Interactive path adds promptBootContractMode() when existing files
  are detected, prompts a/o/s
- Scripted path defaults to append via GAD_ONBOARD_BOOT_MODE env var

FLOW D TEST EXTENSIONS
- First test now also asserts CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md exist with both
  markers and embed the project id, plus 7 critical hook files land
  at .claude/hooks/ (4 js + 3 sh)
- New second test: seeds CLAUDE.md with custom header + stale GAD
  block + custom footer, re-runs installer, asserts stale GAD block
  was replaced in place and both custom sections survived verbatim

20/20 tests green across consumer-init-install, proto-skill-state,
evolution-install-bundle.

DESIGN NOTE (not shipped, discussion material)
.planning/notes/temp-skill-install-and-graphify-2026-04-15.md covers
the next-direction asks from the same operator turn:

- Temporary skill install flow (gad try <skill>) with consent gate,
  ephemeral sandbox, dependency audit, cleanup command — enables
  trialing external skills without full install
- Graphify research summary (skill, python 3.10+, graph.json +
  graph.html + GRAPH_REPORT.md + Obsidian/wiki/Neo4j exports,
  71.5x fewer tokens per query claim, appends to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
  which validates the append pattern shipped in this commit)
- Integration shape: planning-graph.json as site data, backing
  command+K, VCS graph panel, DB viewer
- Skill+graph vs embeddings tradeoff analysis (recommendation:
  graph is primary, embeddings optional secondary)
- Vendor-vs-native decision framing (option A: vendor Graphify as
  skills/gad-graphify/ with Python dep; option B: Node-native
  extractor targeting same graph.json shape) — deferred to trial
  findings
- Five follow-up tasks queued: 42.2-40 (try flow), 42.2-41 (graphify
  trial), 42.2-42 (vendor vs native decision), 42.2-43
  (planning-graph.json pipeline), 42.2-44 (VCS graph panel)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

==> .gad-try/graphify/skill.md <==
---
name: graphify
description: any input (code, docs, papers, images) → knowledge graph → clustered communities → HTML + JSON + audit report
trigger: /graphify
---

# /graphify

Turn any folder of files into a navigable knowledge graph with community detection, an honest audit trail, and three outputs: interactive HTML, GraphRAG-ready JSON, and a plain-language GRAPH_REPORT.md.

## Usage

```
/graphify                                             # full pipeline on current directory → Obsidian vault
/graphify <path>                                      # full pipeline on specific path
/graphify <path> --mode deep                          # thorough extraction, richer INFERRED edges
/graphify <path> --update                             # incremental - re-extract only new/changed files
/graphify <path> --directed                            # build directed graph (preserves edge direction: source→target)
/graphify <path> --whisper-model medium                # use a larger Whisper model for better transcription accuracy
/graphify <path> --cluster-only                       # rerun clustering on existing graph
/graphify <path> --no-viz                             # skip visualization, just report + JSON
/graphify <path> --html                               # (HTML is generated by default - this flag is a no-op)
/graphify <path> --svg                                # also export graph.svg (embeds in Notion, GitHub)
/graphify <path> --graphml                            # export graph.graphml (Gephi, yEd)
/graphify <path> --neo4j                              # generate graphify-out/cypher.txt for Neo4j
/graphify <path> --neo4j-push bolt://localhost:7687   # push directly to Neo4j
/graphify <path> --mcp                                # start MCP stdio server for agent access
/graphify <path> --watch                              # watch folder, auto-rebuild on code changes (no LLM needed)
/graphify <path> --wiki                               # build agent-crawlable wiki (index.md + one article per community)

# Graphify trial via `gad try` — findings

Ran the newly-shipped `gad try` flow (task 42.2-40) against the real
Graphify repository on github to see what the artifact looks like, what
dependencies it declares, and whether integrating it into GAD is a
vendor-or-native decision worth making.

## Command

```sh
gad try https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify --yes
```

## What landed in `.gad-try/graphify/`

33 files, ~200KB. Breakdown:

| Kind | Files |
|---|---|

2. **Branch detection could be smarter.** `gad try` falls back through

"Lineage" remained accurate only for the **GSD upstream reference** (link + YouTube segment), not for the primary UX story. That mismatch persisted across refactors because: (1) **incremental edits** prioritized the prompt payload and media swap over filesystem renames; (2) **no TASK-REGISTRY task** explicitly gated "rename module when semantics change"; (3) **greppable `cid` churn** was deferred to avoid one-shot breakages in the showcase prompt and dev-panel bookmarks.

**GAD loop application:** treat stale public identifiers as **technical debt** the same as an unfinished task — capture in DECISIONS (this entry), rename files + `cid` in one atomic change, update the verbatim showcase string, and **commit** so snapshot and site stay aligned.

    <title>Graphify integration: Option B (native Node extractor) ships first, Option A (vendor Graphify) gated on evidence</title>
    <summary>Task 42.2-41 trialed Graphify end-to-end via the newly-shipped `gad try` flow. Findings in `evals/FINDINGS-2026-04-15-graphify-trial.md` — pipeline proven end-to-end, 33 files staged into `.gad-try/graphify/`, consent gate correctly surfaced the implicit `pip install graphifyy` dependency via body scan.

Two integration paths were considered for bringing a knowledge-graph + visualization primitive into GAD alongside our existing workflow/site/visual-context-system:

**Option A — vendor Graphify as `skills/gad-graphify/`.** Copy the Python-backed skill + its ~15 pipeline modules into the canonical skills tree, accept Python 3.10+ as a GAD optional dependency (precedent: `gad models install` already allows opt-in deps), reuse upstream's multimodal extraction (vision, whisper, PDF) that we would not realistically reproduce in Node.
  Pro: days of work instead of months; multimodal capabilities we cannot match; already-standard `graph.json` shape consumed by Obsidian/Neo4j/Gephi.
  Con: Python in the dep tree contradicts the zero-deps-first memory feedback (though `gad models` sets a lazy-optional precedent); install.js would need a Python environment path (pyenv/uv/pip); upstream release cadence risk.

**Option B — build a GAD-native extractor as `skills/gad-planning-graph/`.** Node-only pipeline that walks `.planning
... [truncated 1030 bytes] ...
t multimodal extraction for an arbitrary folder get it with one command, no framework commitment, cleanup available.
3. **Promote Option A if usage pattern emerges.** If trial volume or specific user requests justify the Python dep, vendor `skills/gad-graphify/` in a follow-up decision. That call becomes evidence-based rather than speculative.

This sequences to the narrowest-first commitment that still unblocks the site integration work while preserving the operator's "try before you commit" request that spawned `gad try` in the first place.</summary>
    <impact>(1) New task `42.2-45` ships Option B as `skills/gad-planning-graph/` — Node-only extractor targeting the `graph.json` shape, scoped to `.planning/` + `skills/` + `decisions/` + `workflows/`. Replaces the previous 42.2-42 placeholder (decision now made, so 42.2-42 becomes the decision doc pointer and is marked done). (2) Task `42.2-43` (planning-graph.json site pipeline + command+K) unblocks and depends on 42.2-45. (3) Task `42.2-44` (VCS graph panel primitive) unblocks and depends on 42.2-43. (4) Task `42.2-40.a` polish items (strip .git after clone, `--branch` flag, interactive consent gate for runtime invocation) deferred to a follow-up but not blocking. (5) `gad try` stays the canonical "trial an external skill" primitive — no framework-level mention of graphify in particular, since any external skill can ride this flow. (6) Planning-graph schema design: nodes typed as `phase | task | decision | skill | workflow | file | requirement`; edges typed as `cites | depends_on | decides | belongs_to | authored_by | supersedes`. Shape must remain compatible with Graphify's `graph.json` so downstream tools (Obsidian, Neo4j, Gephi, the upstream Graphify viewer) can consume it if the operator later wants to. (7) If Option B lands and operators ask for multimodal coverage, the decision to promote Option A becomes a matter of weighing measured demand against Python dep cost — an evidence-based call, not a speculative one.</impact>
  </decision>
</decisions>

      <resolution>Shipped 2026-04-15. New `gad try` top-level command in `bin/gad.cjs` with four subcommands: `stage` (default, bare `gad try <ref>` routes here via an argv injector modelled on `injectRefsListDefault`), `status`, `cleanup`, `help`. Three source ref kinds resolved via `resolveTrySource()`:
        (1) **local-slug** — looks up `skills/<slug>/SKILL.md` in the framework skill catalog
        (2) **local-path** — copies an arbitrary directory containing SKILL.md
        (3) **git-url** — shallow-clones a github/gitlab/ssh url to a tmp dir (tries `null`, `v1`, `main`, `master` branches in order to survive Graphify's non-standard `v1` default), walks up to 3 levels deep for `SKILL.md` or lowercase `skill.md`, then copies the containing dir
        Sandbox layout: `<cwd>/.gad-try/<slug>/{copied source files}/PROVENANCE.md/ENTRY.md`. PROVENANCE records slug, source, kind, staged_on, plus four dependency sections: declared `requires:`, declared `installs:`, implicit installs detected by regex scanning the SKILL.md body for `pip install | npm install -g | npm i -g | pipx install | uv tool install | brew install | curl -fsSL`, and declared `outputs:`. ENTRY.md writes a copy-paste handoff prompt for the user's coding agent: `"Invoke the skill at .gad-try/<slug>/SKILL.md on this directory. Follow its instructions exactly."` Consistent with decis

inputs.new_string:
Tasks 42.2-40 through 42.2-42 shipped same session:
        42.2-40 — `gad try <ref>` CLI with stage/status/cleanup/help subcommands, resolves local-slug / local-path / git-url sources (shallow clone with v1/main/master branch fallback + 3-level recursive SKILL.md walker handles Graphify's non-standard layout), writes PROVENANCE + ENTRY with dual declared-plus-implicit dependency capture (frontmatter `requires:` / `installs:` / `outputs:` keys + body-scan regex for pip/npm/brew/curl), non-destructive staging. 9 new tests in `tests/gad-try.test.cjs`, 29/29 regression green.
        42.2-41 — real Graphify trial via `gad try https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify`. End-to-end flow works against a real third-party repo. 33 files staged, consent gate caught `pip install graphifyy` from body scan. Full findings in `evals/FINDINGS-2026-04-15-graphify-trial.md`: Graphify is a Python-backed pipeline (~15 modules), output is a typed-edge `graph.json` + interactive `graph.html` + `GRAPH_REPORT.md` + Obsidian/Neo4j/Gephi exports, structural queries are LLM-free (good for our command+K), multimodal extraction (vision/whisper/PDF) is out of reach in Node.
        42.2-42 + decision gad-197 — **hybrid, sequenced**. Ship Option B (native Node extractor) first as `skills/gad-planning-graph/` targeting planning XML + skills + decisions + workflows with typed nodes (phase/task/decision/skill/workflow/file/requirement) and typed edges (cites/depends_on/decides/belongs_to/authored_by/supersedes). Keep Option A (vendor Graphify) as evidence-gated opt-in via `gad try` for operators who want multimodal extraction. New task 42.2-45 implements Option B. Tasks 42.2-43 (site data pipeline + command+K integration) and 42.2-44 (VCS graph panel primitive) depend on 42.2-45. Task 42.2-40.a queued for `gad try` polish items (strip .git, `--branch` flag, interactive consent, lowercase skill.md warning).
        Still open for next session: 42.2-45 (native planning-graph extractor), 42.2-43 (site pipeline), 42.2-44 (VCS graph panel). Snapshot EQUIPPED SKILLS pinned refactor + Position B workflow retrofits still queued from earlier.

git add bin/gad.cjs tests/gad-try.test.cjs evals/FINDINGS-2026-04-15-graphify-trial.md .planning/DECISIONS.xml .planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml .planning/STATE.xml && git status --short | grep -E "^[AM]" | head -10 && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
42.2-40/41/42 + gad-197: gad try + graphify trial + hybrid integration decision

Three tasks land together as one coherent unblock of the knowledge-graph
integration direction.

42.2-40 — gad try <ref> CLI primitive
  New top-level `gad try` command with stage/status/cleanup/help
  subcommands. Stages an external skill into .gad-try/<slug>/ without
  touching ~/.claude/skills/ or the project skill catalog.

  Three source ref kinds via resolveTrySource():
    - local-slug: looks up skills/<slug>/SKILL.md in framework catalog
    - local-path: copies an arbitrary dir containing SKILL.md
    - git-url: shallow-clones with v1/main/master branch fallback
      (survives graphify's non-standard v1 default) + 3-level
      recursive walker handles lowercase skill.md

  Sandbox layout:
    .gad-try/<slug>/
      {copied source files}
      PROVENANCE.md   # slug, source, kind, staged_on, dependency audit
      ENTRY.md        # copy-paste handoff prompt for the coding agent

  PROVENANCE dependency audit captures both declared (frontmatter
  `requires:` / `installs:` / `outputs:` keys) and implicit (body-scan
  regex for pip/npm/brew/curl install commands). Consistent with
  decision gad-18: gad try stages and hands off, does not execute.

  Non-destructive by design — staging copies files but runs no
  installs. Cleanup prints (but does not run) the install commands
  it detected so the user can roll back system packages on their own.

  argv injector added alongside the existing injectRefsListDefault +
  injectStateShowDefault pattern so `gad try <ref>` routes correctly
  to `gad try stage <ref>` without colliding with citty subcommand
  dispatch.

  9 new tests in tests/gad-try.test.cjs covering local-slug full
  lifecycle, local-path with fixture skill + dep capture, cleanup
... [truncated 647 bytes] ...
  Full findings at evals/FINDINGS-2026-04-15-graphify-trial.md:
    - Graphify is NOT a thin skill — real work is in ~15 Python
      modules invoked via `pip install graphifyy`
    - Output shape: typed-edge graph.json (EXPLICIT vs INFERRED),
      community detection, graph.html, GRAPH_REPORT.md, wiki/,
      plus optional SVG / GraphML / Neo4j / MCP exports
    - Structural queries (path, neighbors, community) are LLM-free
      and suffice for GAD's command+K / VCS / DB viewer use cases
    - Skill+graph beats embeddings for relationship-typed queries
    - Multimodal extraction (vision/whisper/PDF) is out of reach
      in Node

  Surfaced four minor polish items for gad try logged as 42.2-40.a
  (strip .git from cloned sandboxes, --branch flag, interactive
  consent gate for future runtime-invocation flows, lowercase
  skill.md handling).

42.2-42 + decision gad-197 — hybrid sequenced integration
  Decision: ship Option B (native Node extractor) first, keep
  Option A (vendor Graphify) as evidence-gated opt-in via gad try.

  Option B lands as skills/gad-planning-graph/ (new task 42.2-45)
  targeting .planning/ + skills/ + decisions/ + workflows/ with:
    Node types: phase, task, decision, skill, workflow, file,
                requirement
    Edge types: cites, depends_on, decides, belongs_to,
                authored_by, supersedes

  graph.json shape compatible with Graphify so downstream tools
  (Obsidian, Neo4j, Gephi, upstream viewer) can still consume it
  if the operator ever wants to.

  Option A stays available via `gad try graphify` — already shipped,
  zero framework commitment, cleanup ready. If trial volume
  justifies the Python dep later, vendor decision becomes
  evidence-based rather than speculative.

  Tasks queued: 42.2-45 (native extractor), 42.2-43 (site pipeline
  + command+K), 42.2-44 (VCS graph panel primitive) — unblocks
  the knowledge-graph integration from the backlog into the active
  sprint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

inputs.old_string:
function writeTryEntry(sandboxDir, resolved, skillBody) {
  const { fields } = parseFrontmatterLoose(skillBody);
  const skillName = fields.name || resolved.slug;
  const body = [
    `# ${skillName} — try entry`,
    '',
    `Staged by \`gad try\` on ${new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)}.`,
    '',
    '## How to run it',
    '',
    'Open your coding agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.)',
    `in **${path.dirname(sandboxDir)}** (the parent of .gad-try/) and paste:`,
    '',
    '```',
    `Invoke the skill at .gad-try/${resolved.slug}/SKILL.md on this directory.`,
    `Follow its instructions exactly. When done, tell me what artifacts it produced.`,
    '```',
    '',
    'Or, if the skill ships its own slash command and is wired into your',
    'runtime, use that command directly — the skill body will tell you what',
    'syntax it expects.',
    '',
    '## Where artifacts will land',
    '',
    'Read `PROVENANCE.md` in this sandbox for declared outputs. If the skill',
    'does not declare outputs, it will write to the current working directory —',
    `likely producing files next to \`.gad-try/\`.`,
    '',
    '## When you\'re done',
    '',
    `\`\`\`sh`,
    `gad try cleanup ${resolved.slug}    # remove this sandbox`,
    `\`\`\``,
    '',
    'If the skill installed system packages (pip / npm / brew), cleanup will',
    'print the suggested uninstall commands but will NOT run them — you',
    'decide whether to roll them back.',
    '',
  ].join('\n');
  fs.writeFileSync(path.join(sandboxDir, 'ENTRY.md'), body);
}

inputs.command:
git add bin/gad.cjs tests/gad-try.test.cjs .planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml && git status --short | grep -E "^[AM]" | head && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
42.2-40.a: gad try clipboard auto-populate + cwd docs clarification

Operator follow-up on 42.2-40: asked whether gad try could push the
handoff prompt to the clipboard automatically (yes), and was confused
about where to run gad try when the binary is globally installed
(answer: always cwd-relative — the binary location doesn't matter,
the sandbox lands at <cwd>/.gad-try/<slug>/).

Three changes:

1. buildHandoffPrompt(resolved) extracted as single source of truth
   for the canonical paste text. ENTRY.md, stdout, and the clipboard
   all read from the same function so they can't drift.

2. copyToClipboardSync(text) shells out to the platform-native
   clipboard tool with silent degradation:
     Windows:  clip.exe
     macOS:    pbcopy
     Linux:    wl-copy (Wayland) / xclip / xsel
   Zero new deps — just spawnSync with { input: text }. Returns the
   tool name on success or null. Verified on Windows: staged
   gad-help and "Clipboard: copied via clip" reported, paste
   roundtrip works.

3. stdout after staging now prints sandbox path, ENTRY.md path,
   clipboard status, a "Paste this into your coding agent running
   in <cwd>" banner, the full handoff prompt inline, and the
   cleanup command. `gad try help` rewritten to lead with four
   lines explaining that the sandbox is always cwd-relative so
   global-install users understand where to cd first. ENTRY.md
   gains a "Where the sandbox is" section + clipboard mention.

Tests: 3 new assertions covering the cwd docs, clipboard mention
in ENTRY.md, and stdout-prints-prompt path. 9/9 green.

Deferred to 42.2-40.b: strip .git from cloned sandboxes, --branch
flag, interactive consent for future runtime-invocation, lowercase
skill.md warning. None block the clipboard/cwd work that actually
answers the operator's questions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\vendor\get-anything-done\.planning\ROADMAP.xml","content":"  <phase id="44.5">
    <title>Project Editor + Brood Editor — local-dev gamified species/brood/generation workspace</title>
    <goal>After the marketplace lands (phase 44), build a local-dev-only Project Editor surface that contains the Brood Editor as its species-level inner surface. The editor is the operator's workspace for authoring projects, defining recipes, running species, and viewing the resulting broods of generations. Discuss-phase 2026-04-15 locked terminology + visual primitives + draft/published model in decision gad-189. Vocabulary: **Recipe** = reusable template for producing a species. **Species** = recipe attached to a concrete project. **Generation** = one concrete run of a species. **Brood** = the accumulated population of generations for a species. **Bestiary** = UI view of every species + its brood across a project. Scope: (1) **New editor route** `/projects/[id]/edit` (or `/project-editor/[id]`), dev-gated via `NODE_ENV=development`. NOT the existing `/projects/[...id]` catch-all — that remains a read-only listing detail surface. (2) **Split viewport** — left pane: bestiary grid or React Flow graph of species + broods; right pane: iframe live preview of the currently-selected generation's preserved build; bottom pane: config inspector with minecraft-style inventory grid for the species's gad.json/recipe. (3) **Visual primitives**: animated trait bars, radar chart per species (6-8 trait axes, prototype), visual diff tree with glow on changed traits between generations, minecraft inventory grid for config, in-game bestiary cards. Gamified feel comes from primitives, not a literal game engine. (4) **Visual Context System is mandatory** per decision gad-186/187 + the operator's hard requirement: every section gets an explicit `SiteSection cid="*-site-section"` literal, every interactive element is grep-able, and the panel + modal-footer CRUD loop must work on every surface before the feature is considered done. No exceptions for game-styled components. (5) **Draft/published model** (decision gad-189): new `published: false` boolean on `project.json` (reserved on `species.json`). `/project-market` filters to published; `/projects/[...id]` listing detail shows both; editor shows all. (6) **Dev-server command bridge** (permissive first, allowlist hardening pass second) behind a dev-only API route. (7) **File-system adapter** for round-trip editing of `project.json`, `species.json`, `gad.json`, per-species overrides. (8) **Recipe CRUD** — operator defines recipes inside project data; running a recipe produces a generation that accumulates in the species's brood. (9) **Bestiary view** — read-only history of generations rendered as bestiary cards. (10) **Data source update for existing listing** — `project-skills-scope-section-site-section` on `/projects/[...id]` stays on the listing but its data changes from project-level skill catalog to brood/generation-aggregated skill usage. Dogfood target: `projects/project-editor/project-editor/` (scaffolded 2026-04-15 via task 44-14 with baseline species inheriting from app-forge v1). Explicit non-goals: no Vercel-hosted mutation path (decision gad-170), no sprite-based game engine, no split into separate `/project-editor` and `/species-editor` routes (the editor is one surface with inline expansion).</goal>
    <status>planned</status>
    <depends>44</depends>
  </phase>
  <phase id="45">
    <title>Full site rebrand — visual identity, copy pass, hero rewrite around evolutionary model</title>","numLines":8,"startLine":291,"totalLines":305}}

    <title>Skills are the genes in the DNA — install/try/proto/shed/promote collapse into one lifecycle, surfaced as the DNA Editor inside the Project Editor</title>
    <summary>Operator (2026-04-15) made the biological metaphor explicit and the scattered commands suddenly line up:

**Genes = skills.** A skill (SKILL.md + workflow) is the atomic hereditary unit. The project's `.claude/skills/` dir is the DNA — the permanent, integrated set of genes the organism expresses by default.

**`gad try` is epigenetic expression.** Staging a s
... [truncated 4264 bytes] ...
score data; **44.5-13** action row wiring through the 44.5-02 dev bridge (try/promote/shed/cleanup/copy-prompt/preview buttons each running the corresponding CLI command); **44.5-14** iframe right-pane integration for trial artifacts (auto-detect `graph.html`, generic `index.html`, or a SKILL.md-declared `outputs:` path); **44.5-15** command+K integration via the 42.2-43 planning-graph so operators can type "try X", "promote Y", "shed Z" as shortcuts. (3) New task **42.2-46** writes the verb-collapse doc: references/skill-lifecycle.md explains the two verbs (express/integrate) against the three states (expressed/mutation/integrated), maps every existing CLI to the new verb model, and deprecates `install-skill` global as the default path (backward compatible — shim stays, but docs route new users through `gad try` + `gad evolution promote`). (4) `scaffold-visual-context-surface` skill gets its first real use — applies its 6-step checklist to every pane in the DNA Editor build. This is also the success criterion for the skill: if the DNA Editor scaffolds cleanly using it, the skill is validated against real work, not just the phase 44.5 candidate it was drafted from. (5) Graphify integration via `gad try graphify` inside the DNA Editor iframe may supersede or replace task 42.2-45 (native planning-graph extractor) depending on what the operator sees when they run the Graphify trial live — that decision stays evidence-gated and is noted as a superseding-candidate for gad-197. (6) All four gene-state dirs (`.claude/skills/`, `.gad-try/`, `.planning/proto-skills/`, shed records) become canonical read sources for the DNA Editor pane — documented in references/skill-shape.md as part of 42.2-46. (7) Visual Context System cid naming contract: every gene row uses `dna-editor-gene-<slug>-site-section`; every state-group header uses `dna-editor-state-<state>-site-section`; the panel shell uses `dna-editor-pane-site-section`. All literal, grep-able, deterministic on slug.</impact>
  </decision>
</decisions>

Ships with empty state groups — the list population is 44.5-12's scope. Dev-panel badge "DNA Editor (dev mode)" visible. Integrates with 42.2-43's command+K global search to add gene-lifecycle shortcuts: typing "try <ref>" triggers the same flow as the Try button; "promote <slug>" runs evolution promote; "shed <slug>" runs evolution shed; "preview <slug>" loads the trial artifact into the iframe. Map every existing CLI command to its lifecycle role: `gad try`, `gad try cleanup`, `gad evolution install`, `gad evolution promote`, `gad evolution shed`, `gad evolution discard`, `gad skill promote-folder`, `gad install` (local), `gad install --global`. Deprecate `gad install --global` as the default first-encounter path for new skills (route new users through `gad try` + `gad evolution promote` instead). Backward compatible — no CLI changes, docs only. Update `references/skill-shape.md` §1 and AGENTS.md loop section to cite the new lifecycle model. Per decision gad-198.</goal>
      <keywords>docs,skill-lifecycle,verb-collapse,references,gad-198</keywords>
    </task>
  </phase>
</task-registry>

inputs.command:
git commit -m "gad-198 + 44.5-11..15 + 42.2-46: DNA Editor + skill lifecycle unification

Operator made the biological metaphor explicit: skills are the genes
in the DNA, gad try is epigenetic expression, proto-skills are
mutations, promote is integration. Scattered install/try/proto/shed/
promote commands collapse into one lifecycle with two verbs (express
/ integrate) against three states (expressed / mutation / integrated).

DECISION gad-198 captures the framing + DNA Editor as the unified
surface that subsumes skill trial / promote / shed / install management.

PHASE 44.5 GOAL EXTENDED to Project Editor + Brood Editor + DNA Editor.
No new phase number — DNA Editor is a sibling pane alongside Bestiary,
consistent with editor family naming. Roadmap scope item (11) added
covering the four state groups (DNA / Expressed / Mutations / Shed),
cid naming contract, and dev-bridge action model.

NEW TASKS
  44.5-11  scaffold DNA Editor via scaffold-visual-context-surface
           skill (first real application, fractal closure on the
           42.2-13 evolution pipeline proof earlier today)
  44.5-12  list population from .claude/skills/, .gad-try/,
           .planning/proto-skills/, shed data
  44.5-13  action row wiring through 44.5-02 dev bridge
  44.5-14  iframe preview for trial artifacts (graph.html etc)
  44.5-15  command+K gene-lifecycle shortcuts
  42.2-46  verb collapse docs in references/skill-lifecycle.md

CLEAN BOUNDARY. Decision captured, tasks queued, rehydratable from
snapshot. No code this commit — just the planning moves. Next session
starts on 44.5-11 with full context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>"

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\CLAUDE.md","content":"# Communication style — read once

**Spec:** `vendor/get-anything-done/references/communication-style.md`

Military SITREP. Tables for structured state. Set root once, deltas only.
No narration, no trailing summaries, no emoji. Long is fine if distinct;
short is wrong if vague. Goal is information density per token, not minimal
tokens. Inherited as the default by every GAD project — override only with
explicit project-level reason.

# Startup

**First action in every session:** run the GAD startup + snapshot for whichever project you're working on.

```sh
node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs startup --projectid <id>
```

`gad startup` prints the session contract and delegates to a full snapshot. One command gets you the whole session. If you prefer explicit snapshot:

```sh
node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs snapshot --projectid <id>
```

Common project IDs: `get-anything-done`, `global`, `grime-time-site`, `repub-builder`, `mb-cli-framework`, `gad-manuscript`.

If the user doesn't specify a project, ask. If working on the GAD framework itself, use `get-anything-done`.

Do NOT manually read planning files. The snapshot gives you state, roadmap, tasks, decisions, and file refs in one low-token command. Pull more detail with individual commands (`gad tasks`, `gad decisions`, `gad phases`, `gad state`) only when needed.

**Find skills fast:**

```sh
gad skill list --paths           # full inventory with absolute paths
gad skill show <id>              # name, description, resolved workflow path
gad skill show <id> --body       # full SKILL.md + workflow file contents
```

# Static vs active info (decision gad-195)

Session context splits into two buckets:

- **Static info** (loaded once, never re-pulled): skill catalog, workflow specs, decision history, references, roadmap phase structure.
- **Active info** (changes as you work): STATE.xml `<next-action>`, current phase, open sprint tasks.

Full snapshot loads both at session start. After that, use `gad snapshot --mode=active` for mid-session state refreshes (~3900 tokens, ~50% lighter). Re-running the full snapshot mid-session is wasteful — the agent already has the static parts in working memory.

# After auto-compact

Trust your working memory. The planning docs are durable — if you genuinely need fresh active state, run `gad snapshot --mode=active`. Do NOT reflexively re-run the full snapshot; per decision gad-195, that is wasteful context re-loading. Default behavior is to never stop work mid-task and never restart unilaterally (decision gad-17).","numLines":50,"startLine":0,"totalLines":139}}

inputs.new_string:
`gad startup` auto-creates a session, prints the session contract, and delegates to a full snapshot with `--session <id>`. The snapshot footer prints `Reuse: --session <id>` — **save that session id** and pass it on all subsequent snapshot calls:

```sh
node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs snapshot --projectid <id> --session <session-id>
```

With `--session`, the snapshot auto-downgrades to `mode=active` when static context was already delivered, saving ~50% tokens. You can also set `GAD_SESSION_ID=<id>` as an env var. Explicit `--mode=full` overrides the auto-downgrade.

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:\Users\benja\Documents\custom_portfolio\CLAUDE.md","content":"```

This copies code/planning to `evals/<project>/v<N>/run/` and build to
`apps/portfolio/public/evals/<project>/v<N>/`. Without this step, the agent's work is
lost. `tests/eval-preservation.test.cjs` will fail the build if you skip it.

Verify with `gad eval verify`. See `gad:eval-run` skill for the full procedure.

ent* task degrades even though the old turns are technically still there.
4. Context window is finite. Hitting the wall mid-task forces an unplanned auto-compact that is lossier than a planned restart.

**When a restart suggestion is appropriate:**
- A phase or large task just landed and its decisions are now captured in planning docs (rehydration story is good)
- The next chunk of work is clearly scoped and self-contained (no dangling in-head state)
- Topic is switching (e.g., "we're done with phase 43, now let's look at phase 44 marketplace design")
- The agent's context is past ~60-70% and the next task is independent
- Auto-compact is imminent â€” a planned restart now beats a forced compaction mid-task

**When a restart is NOT appropriate:**
- Mid-task with unwritten state in the agent's head
- During an investigation where the trail of "what we ruled out" carries weight
- When the next thing depends on subtle context from the last few turns
- Any time the user is in flow and a restart would interrupt their thinking

**How to suggest:** loosely, with one-line reasoning ("phase done, context ~70%, next chunk is mechanical and rehydratable from snapshot â€” restart is reasonable here, or I can keep going"). The user decides. Never push, never restart unilaterally. The default still leans toward continuing â€” restart is the exception, not the norm.</summary>
  <impact>Agents work through auto-compact by default and never restart unilaterally. They MAY suggest a restart loosely at clean planning boundaries when context is filling and the next chunk has a good rehydration story via `gad snapshot`. The user always decides. gad-04 (decisions in planning docs) is still what makes both auto-compact and planned restarts work â€” without durable docs, no rehydration story exists at all.</impact>
</decision>

name: scaffold-visual-context-surface
description: >-
  Scaffold a new dev-only UI surface (editor, inspector, admin pane,
  modal stack) under the Visual Context System mandate. Triggers on
  "scaffold a new editor", "build a gated dev route", "add a Project
  Editor pane", "new admin surface", or any task whose acceptance
  requires `SiteSection cid="..."` literals, Visual Context Panel
  registration, and a modal-footer CRUD round-trip before feature work
  starts. Enforces the six-step scaffolding-first checklist: dev gate
  first, then enumerate panes and cids, emit literals, register
  identities, round-trip one prompt, only then build features. Prevents
  the retrofit tax of adding Visual Context coverage after features
  already exist. See decisions gad-186 + gad-187 and the 2026-04-15
  Visual Context mandatory feedback for the source constraints.
status: stable
workflow: workflows/scaffold-visual-context-surface.md
---

# scaffold-visual-context-surface

Scaffolding-first discipline for new dev-only UI surfaces. Every new
editor pane, admin route, or inspector modal in this repo must ship with
complete Visual Context coverage before any feature affordance is built
— stable literal cids on every interactive element, Visual Context Panel
identities registered, and at least one modal-footer CRUD round-trip
green against a real cid.

This proto-skill captures the checklist as a reusable pattern so future
gated-editor work does not re-derive it from scratch or ship drift.

**Workflow:** [./workflow.md](./workflow.md)

## When this fires

- Task acceptance mentions `SiteSection cid` or Visual Context Panel
- Building a new `/<surface>` route behind a dev-mode gate
- Adding an editor, inspector, admin console, or modal stack
- Cleanup pass on a surface that skipped the mandate ("retrofit")

## When NOT to use this

- You're adding a single element to an already-compliant surface — use
  `gad-visual-context-panel-identities` directly.
- You're building a production-facing page (no dev gate) — the gate is
  part of this skill's contract; different rules apply.
- You're refactoring internals with no visible surface change.

## Provenance

Drafted by `create-proto-skill` from
`.planning/candidates/phase-44.5-project-editor-brood-editor-local-dev-ga/CANDIDATE.md`
on 2026-04-15 as the first end-to-end run of the evolution pipeline
(task 42.2-13). Selection pressure: 29 (phase 44.5, 15 tasks, 7
crosscuts). See PROVENANCE.md for source metadata and reasoning about
what the skill abstracts.

## Related

- `gad-visual-context-panel-identities` — identity registration mechanics
- Decisions gad-186, gad-187, gad-189, gad-191
- Feedback memory `feedback_visual_context_system_mandatory.md`

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/c/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/workflows/scaffold-visual-context-surface.md","content":"# scaffold-visual-context-surface — workflow

The procedure an agent follows when scaffolding a new dev-only UI surface
(editor, inspector, admin pane, modal stack) under the Visual Context
System mandate.

## Why this exists

Every new GUI surface in this repo must ship with complete Visual Context
coverage: stable `cid`s on every interactive element, Visual Context Panel
identities registered, and at least one modal-footer CRUD prompt
round-tripping against a real cid *before* any feature affordance goes in.
Skipping the scaffolding-first discipline produces surfaces that are
invisible to the Visual Context Panel and ungrep-able — and the
retrofitting cost is much higher than doing it right the first time
(decisions gad-186 + gad-187, and the 2026-04-15 Visual Context feedback
memory).

This workflow is the checklist. Follow it in order. Do not build any
editor affordance (drag-drop, inline editors, data wiring) until every
step is green.

## Step 1 — gate the surface

Add the route or pane under a `NODE_ENV === 'development'` guard at
module-load time. Show a visible "(dev mode)" badge in the surface itself
so the operator can see the gate is active.

```tsx
if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'development') {
  return <NotFoundInProduction />;
}
```

The gate is non-negotiable even when the surface is "obviously" safe —
the Project Editor command bridge (phase 44.5-02) learned this the hard
way. Reject prod at the boundary so a wide-open dev surface can never
ship.

## Step 2 — enumerate panes and assign cids

Before writing any JSX, list every distinct region of the surface:

- Each viewport pane (left/right/bottom in a split view)
- Each toolbar, header, footer
- Each interactive element group (button clusters, form sections)
- Each row/card/slot primitive that will eventually be virtualized

For 
... [truncated 2537 bytes] ...
he backing data source.

The round-trip is load-bearing proof that downstream feature work will
have a working CRUD surface instead of an inert one.

## Step 6 — only now build features

With the scaffold + cid coverage + round-trip green, feature work begins.
Every new interactive element added from this point also follows steps 3
and 4 (literal cid + registration) — drift is how surfaces decay out of
the Visual Context System.

Acceptance for the scaffold phase (step 6 entry gate):

- [ ] Dev gate rejects prod at module load
- [ ] Every pane emits a literal cid
- [ ] Every cid registered with the Visual Context Panel
- [ ] At least one modal-footer CRUD round-trip green
- [ ] Dev-panel badge visible
- [ ] tsc clean

## Failure modes

- **Cid is a computed string.** Grep can't find it. Rewrite as a literal
  or add a prototype-literal comment alongside the computed version.
- **Feature work started before the round-trip.** Stop. Finish step 5
  first. The round-trip is cheaper to fix against an empty scaffold than
  against a scaffold with half-built features.
- **Dev gate added "later".** Added late means a window existed where the
  surface could ship to prod. Revert and reland with the gate at the
  top of the module.
- **"Just one" unregistered element.** Becomes five. Becomes twenty.
  Register at the moment of introduction, not at a "cleanup pass" later.
- **Cid families without prototype comments.** Future agents searching
  for an example of the pattern find nothing. Always leave one
  literal-string reference grep can find.

## Related

- `gad-visual-context-panel-identities` — identity registration mechanics
- Decision gad-186 — dev-panel copy tokens must be source-searchable
- Decision gad-187 — Visual Context Panel naming contract (cid first)
- Phase 44.5 task 44.5-01 — the scaffold-first task that drove this skill
- Feedback memory `feedback_visual_context_system_mandatory.md` (2026-04-15)

inputs.description: Explore GAD site project editor prerequisites
inputs.prompt:
Explore the GAD site codebase at `vendor/get-anything-done/site/` to understand what exists for the Project Editor work. I need:

1. **Routing structure**: What routes exist under `site/app/`? Specifically look for `/projects/` routes — there should be a listing page at `/projects/[...id]` or similar.

2. **Existing project-related components**: Search for any components related to projects, project-market, listings, etc. under `site/components/` or `site/app/`.

3. **Visual Context System components**: Find VCS-related components — HoverCard, panels, etc. The DNA Editor will be scaffolded using a `scaffold-visual-context-surface` pattern.

4. **Dev bridge or API routes**: Check `site/app/api/` for any existing dev-only API routes.

5. **Generated data files**: Check `site/lib/` for `*.generated.ts` files — especially eval-data, catalog, etc. that feed project listings.

6. **The scaffold-visual-context-surface skill**: Check `vendor/get-anything-done/skills/scaffold-visual-context-surface/SKILL.md` and `vendor/get-anything-done/workflows/scaffold-visual-context-surface.md` for the scaffolding pattern.

Be thorough — I need file paths and a sense of the component architecture.

PHASE 10 in-flight. Phases 07, 08, 09 SHIPPED this session (see ROADMAP.xml).

**Phase 10 status**: `gad site compile/serve` CLI landed (commit monorepo `695231a`, GAD submodule `95d7e53`). Works end-to-end on magicborn locally:
  node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs site compile --root projects/magicborn --out projects/magicborn/dist/site
  node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs site serve --skip-compile --out <dir>
All top-level routes return 200, client-nav RSC payloads serve, 404.html fallback works.

**TWO OPEN ITEMS for next session:**

1. **NAV + HOVERCARD BROKEN ON GAD'S OWN LIVE SITE (or compiled output of GAD root)** — user reported after phase 10: "hovercard doesnt pop up even... I guess we need dynamic routing". UNCLEAR whether they tested:
   - get-anything-done.vercel.app (live deploy — pre-existing bug)
   - Local `gad site compile --root vendor/get-anything-done --out ...` (compile output — my bug)
   FIRST STEP: ask user which URL they tested. If live: investigate why hydration is broken on deployed vercel build. If compiled: the JS chunks load fine per my probes, but client hydration may be failing because extracted static files lack Next.js runtime pieces (e.g. `_next/data/*.json` buildManifest, app-paths-manifest, route-modules). Check `vendor/get-anything-done/site/.next/` for what else needs copying to `_next/`. Likely fix: also copy `.next/build-manifest.json`, `.next/app-path-routes-manifest.json`, `.next/BUILD_ID`, and any `_next/data/` files into the extract.

2. **MAGICBORN-SPECIFIC SCOPING** — user confirmed magicborn should have:
   - KEEP: /planning (as home), system tab, catalog (merged into system tab)
   - DROP: landing page, /evals, /project-market, evaluations tab, /theory, /play, /emergent, /skeptic
   - Home page: /planning replaces the GAD hero landing
   DESIGN (proposed, not started): add `[site]` section to `.planning/gad-config.toml` with per-project fields:
     include_pages = ["planning", "decisions", "skills", ...]
     nav_tabs = ["system", "catalog"]   # tabs shown in Nav
     home_page = "planning"              # which route becomes index.html
   Implementation: build-site-data.mjs reads and writes site-config.generated.ts; Nav component reads it to conditionally render tabs; extractPlanningSite filters file list based on include_pages.

**What's DONE in phase 10 (don't re-do):**
- `gad site compile/serve` CLI commands (bin/gad.cjs)
- lib/site-compile.cjs with compileSite + serveStatic + extractPlanningSite
- GAD_PROJECT_ROOT + GAD_PROJECT_ID env vars in build-site-data.mjs
- Skip computeSelfEval when GAD_PROJECT_ROOT is set
- All pre-rendered routes copied from .next/server/app/
- 404.html fallback, .html sibling priority in serveStatic, .rsc mime type

**Session phase log** (what shipped this session):
- Phase 07 commit 1b41e8d — stabilize foundations (lockfiles, workspace sync, repo-planner delete)
- Phase 08 commit 8cbf526 — planning sink moved to monorepo-root docs/, GAD CLI gets --only/--ignore filters
- Phase 09 commit a756800 — vendor/gad-manuscript → projects/magicborn, books+in-world consolidated
- Phase 10 commit 6cb1d3a + 695231a — gad site compile/serve

Run `gad snapshot --projectid global` after compaction.

inputs.prompt:
You're working in the GAD framework repo at `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/`. This is a planning/eval framework for AI coding agents.

## Context

GAD previously trialed Graphify (https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify) via `gad try`. The trial staged 33 files. Graphify is a Python-backed knowledge-graph pipeline that produces typed-edge `graph.json` + interactive `graph.html` + `GRAPH_REPORT.md` + Obsidian/Neo4j/Gephi exports. Structural queries are LLM-free.

The operator has now decided:
1. **Accept the Python dependency** — Graphify is no longer a trial. Full install.
2. **`gad query` CLI command** — new command to query the project graph, offloading context-heavy lookups from the LLM to the graph
3. **Context savings** — we want to measure how much context tokens we save by using `gad query` vs full snapshot/file reads
4. The installer (`bin/install.js`, ~6000 lines) should help consumers install Python/pip dependencies needed for Graphify

## Your tasks (in order)

### Task 1: Promote Graphify from trial to full install
- Check `.gad-try/` for the existing Graphify staging
- Run `node bin/gad.cjs try status` to see current trial state
- Move/install Graphify properly. Check if there's an `evolution promote` or similar path, or if we need a manual install into the vendor/tools area
- The Graphify Python package needs to be callable from Node. Figure out the integration path (child_process spawn of `graphify` CLI, or Python subprocess)

### Task 2: Build `gad query` CLI command
- Add a `query` subcommand to `bin/gad.cjs`
- It should:
  - Accept a natural language or structured query about the project
  - Run Graphify's structural query engine against the project's `graph.json`
  - Return results formatted for agent consumption (compact, token-efficient)
  - Support `--projectid` like other gad commands
- Example queries: "what tasks depend on 44.5-02", "which skills were used in phase 42.2", "show decisions that cite phase 44"

### Task 3: Graph generation pipeline
- `gad graph` or `gad graphify` command to generate/regenerate the graph from `.planning/` XML files + `skills/` + `workflows/`
- Output to `.planning/graph.json` and `.planning/graph.html`
- Should run automatically as part of `gad snapshot` or as a post-commit hook option

### Task 4: Context savings measurement
- Design a simple test: run a typical lookup (e.g., "what are the open tasks in phase 44.5") via:
  a. Full snapshot (measure token count)
  b. `gad query` (measure token count of result)
- Report the ratio

### Important references
- Trial findings: `evals/FINDINGS-2026-04-15-graphify-trial.md`
- Decision gad-197: hybrid approach (now superseded — full Graphify accepted)
- Task 42.2-45: was the native Node extractor task (can be cancelled/superseded now)
- Existing gad CLI: `bin/gad.cjs` — look at how other subcommands are structured
- The `.gad-try/` directory has the staged Graphify files from the trial

### Planning doc updates
After implementation, update:
- `.planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml` — mark relevant tasks, add new ones
- `.planning/DECISIONS.xml` — add decision for full Graphify acceptance (supersedes the hybrid approach in gad-197)
- `.planning/STATE.xml` — update next-action

### GAD CLI pattern
Run `node bin/gad.cjs snapshot --projectid get-anything-done` at start to get full context. Look at how existing subcommands (state, tasks, snapshot, try) are structured in `bin/gad.cjs` to match the pattern for the new `query` and `graph` commands.

Work in the GAD submodule directory: `C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/`

Commit your work when each task is done. Use the standard commit format with Co-Authored-By.

- The agent's context is past ~60-70% and the next task is independent
- Auto-compact is imminent â€” a planned restart now beats a forced compaction mid-task

**When a restart is NOT appropriate:**
- Mid-task with unwritten state in the agent's head
- During an investigation where the trail of "what we ruled out" carries weight
- When the next thing depends on subtle context from the last few turns
- Any time the user is in flow and a restart would interrupt their thinking

**How to suggest:** loosely, with one-line reasoning ("phase done, context ~70%, next chunk is mechanical and rehydratable from snapshot â€” restart is reasonable here, or I can keep going"). The user decides. Never push, never restart unilaterally. The default still leans toward continuing â€” restart is the exception, not the norm.</summary>
  <impact>Agents work through auto-compact by default and never restart unilaterally. They MAY suggest a restart loosely at clean planning boundaries when context is filling and the next chunk has a good rehydration story via `gad snapshot`. The user always decides. gad-04 (decisions in planning docs) is still what makes both auto-compact and planned restarts work â€” without durable docs, no rehydration story exists at all.</impact>
</decision>
<decision id="gad-18">
  <title>The GAD loop is simple: snapshot â†’ work â†’ update docs â†’ commit</title>
  <summary>The canonical GAD loop for any agent, any coding tool:
1. `gad snapshot --projectid <id>` â€” one command, full context. No reading 15 files manually.
2. Pick one task from TASK-REGISTRY.xml (status=planned).
3. Implement it.
4. Update TASK-REGISTRY.xml (mark done), STATE.xml (next-action), DECISIONS.xml (if new decisions).
5. Commit.
No staged tries in this directory.

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"C:/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done/evals/FINDINGS-2026-04-15-graphify-trial.md","content":"---
title: Graphify trial via `gad try` — first look at the knowledge-graph skill
date: 2026-04-15
tasks: [42.2-41]
blocks: [42.2-42, 42.2-43, 42.2-44]
status: complete
---

# Graphify trial via `gad try` — findings

Ran the newly-shipped `gad try` flow (task 42.2-40) against the real
Graphify repository on github to see what the artifact looks like, what
dependencies it declares, and whether integrating it into GAD is a
vendor-or-native decision worth making.

## Command

```sh
gad try https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify --yes
```

## What landed in `.gad-try/graphify/`

33 files, ~200KB. Breakdown:

| Kind | Files |
|---|---|
| Skill entry points | `skill.md` (canonical, lowercase), `skill-aider.md`, `skill-claw.md`, `skill-codex.md`, `skill-copilot.md` — one per coding agent |
| Python pipeline modules | `analyze.py`, `benchmark.py`, `build.py`, `cache.py`, `cluster.py`, `detect.py`, `export.py`, `extract.py`, `hooks.py`, `ingest.py`, `manifest.py`, `report.py`, `security.py`, `serve.py`, and more — this is the actual graph extraction engine |
| gad try scaffolding | `PROVENANCE.md`, `ENTRY.md` (written by `gad try`) |

Key observation: **Graphify is not a "thin" skill.** The SKILL.md is a
short trigger doc, but the real work is in ~15 Python modules that ship
alongside it. Invoking the skill downloads the whole pipeline and
delegates to the Python package `graphifyy`. This is important for the
vendor-vs-native decision.

## Consent gate behavior

`gad try` correctly surfaced the implicit install command via body scan:

```
Implicit installs found in SKILL.md body:
  ? pip install graphifyy
```

No `installs:` frontmatter key — the skill author relied on prose
instructions. The body-scan fallback caught it anyway, which validates
the dual declared-plus-implicit approach in `gad try`'s consent 
... [truncated 6790 bytes] ...
decision
   becomes evidence-based rather than speculative.

This sequences to the narrowest-first commitment that still unblocks
the site integration tasks (42.2-43, 42.2-44). Option B goes in the
active sprint; Option A waits for demand.

## Gaps in `gad try` surfaced by this trial

Minor polish items, not blockers:

1. **Graphify uses lowercase `skill.md` (not SKILL.md).** `gad try`'s
   recursive walker handles this via case-aware prefs, but this is
   worth noting — the uniform shape contract in `references/skill-shape.md`
   says `SKILL.md` uppercase is canonical, but real-world external
   skills don't uniformly follow that.
2. **Branch detection could be smarter.** `gad try` falls back through
   `[null, v1, main, master]` which worked for graphify (v1), but a
   repo that uses `develop` or a feature branch wouldn't be caught.
   Worth a `--branch <name>` flag for explicit selection.
3. **No cleanup of the .git dir in the clone.** After staging, the
   sandbox contains the full .git/ tree from the shallow clone, which
   is wasted space. Worth an `fs.rmSync(.git)` pass after the copy.
4. **Consent gate is print-only.** Explicit confirmation prompt was
   descoped from the first cut (non-destructive staging makes it
   optional) but for future runtime-invocation flows where the skill
   actually runs, an interactive `y/N` gate is needed.

None block the 42.2-41 findings. Logged as candidate refinements for
42.2-40.a follow-up.

## Verdict

Pipeline proven end-to-end: `gad try <github-url>` → sandbox staged →
PROVENANCE + ENTRY + full source tree present → consent gate correctly
flagged the implicit `pip install graphifyy` → cleanup command
available.

Operator can now trial any external skill in any GAD project without
touching the canonical skill catalog or global runtime dirs.

Next move: Option B (`skills/gad-planning-graph/`) in the active
sprint, Option A (vendor Graphify) deferred as evidence-gated.

<decision id="gad-199">
    <title>Graphify accepted as full dependency — supersedes hybrid approach (gad-197)</title>
    <date>2026-04-16</date>
    <summary>Accept Graphify (Python) as the real graph backend, not just a trial. Three deliverables: (1) full install (not gad-try), (2) `gad query` CLI command for token-efficient graph lookups, (3) `gad graph` to regenerate. The installer (bin/install.js) packages or helps install Python/pip for consumers. Supersedes gad-197's "hybrid, sequenced" approach — Option A (vendor Graphify) becomes the primary path; Option B (native Node extractor, task 42.2-45) is cancelled.</summary>
    <impact>Task 42.2-45 (native Node planning-graph extractor) superseded. New tasks for Graphify full install, gad query, gad graph, and context-savings measurement. Installer gains Python runtime bootstrapping for consumers.</impact>
  </decision>

  <decision id="gad-200">
    <title>Post-shed cleanup pass: review code touched by shed skills, remove dead code, re-graphify</title>
    <date>2026-04-16</date>
    <summary>After any evolution shed cycle, the codebase must be reviewed for dead code left behind by the shed skills. Run Graphify to regenerate the graph post-shed, then query for orphaned references. This is a mandatory step in the evolution lifecycle, not optional cleanup.</summary>
    <impact>Evolution shed workflow gains a cleanup gate. References/skill-lifecycle.md must document this as step N+1 after shed.</impact>
  </decision>

</decisions>

<decisions>

  <decision id="gad-92">
    <title>docs/ is GSD upstream â€” archive it, don't rewrite it</title>
    <summary>User review 2026-04-10: "these docs do not describe gad, they describe GSD, which GAD is based off of, this is a fork of GSD." The docs/ directory (62 files referencing GSD across ARCHITECTURE.md, AGENTS.md, COMMANDS.md, CLI-TOOLS.md, CONFIGURATION.md, USER-GUIDE.md, FEATURES.md, context-budget.md, context-monitor.md, workflow-discuss-mode.md, superpowers/, and 4 language translations) is GSD upstream documentation that shipped with the fork. NONE of it describes GAD. Policy: (1) Move docs/ to docs-gsd-upstream/ as an explicit archive with a README noting it's prior art from the upstream fork, not GAD documentation. (2) GAD's documentation is the SITE (get-anything-done.vercel.app) â€” /methodology, /standards, /decisions, /glossary, /contribute, /skeptic, etc. We do NOT maintain a parallel docs/ folder. (3) The GSD docs are valuable as reference for what the upstream was doing (cited on /lineage), and some content may inspire future GAD features â€” but we evaluate those features through our eval framework rather than inherit GSD's claims. (4) The GSD agent patterns (18 specialized gsd-* agents described in docs/AGENTS.md) are useful prior art for the agent-parallelization hypothesis (gad-93).</summary>
    <impact>(1) Quick rename: git mv docs/ docs-gsd-upstream/. Add a README.md at the top explaining the provenance. (2) Any site page currently linking to docs/ files updates to docs-gsd-upstream/. (3) The prebuild does NOT scan docs/ for anything â€” confirmed by grep. (4) No site content changes needed â€” the site is self-contained.</impact>
  </decision>

    <title>Agent-parallelization hypothesis â€” does multi-agent work improve outcom

+ coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Cline, OpenCode, Copilot, Continue, and more) and handles add/list/find/remove/update/check. It respects the `.agents/skills/` convention from gad-80 and works with GitHub shorthand (`owner/repo`), full URLs, and local paths. Policy: GAD does NOT reimplement skill installation. Users install GAD's workspace skills via `npx skills add MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done`. GAD's own `find-skills` skill becomes a thin methodology wrapper around `npx skills find`. The workspace-skill copy pass I added to `install.js` in task 22-46 remains in place as a fallback for users who prefer one-command install via `gad install`, but the canonical recommendation on /contribute + /standards is `npx skills`. The effect is that GAD becomes "a barrel for tools a user would want" â€” we aggregate skill research, methodology, hypothesis testing, and eval framework, and delegate skill installation to the open ecosystem tool that already solved the distribution problem.</summary>
    <impact>(1) The `find-skills` skill (new this turn per gad-73 triumvirate) is authored as a methodology wrapper around `npx skills find`. It does NOT implement its own search logic. (2) /contribute updated (queued) to tell users "install GAD skills via `npx skills add MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done`" as the first-class path. (3) /standards updated (queued) to cite vercel-labs/skills alongside the Anthropic PDF and agentskills.io. (4) The install.js workspace-skills copy (22-46) is retained but labelled as fallback â€” the canonical path is npx skills. (5) /security page gains a new mitigation: skills installed via npx skills inherit the ecosystem's trust posture (public repos, git provenance, hash-pinning). (6) Substantial simplification â€” GAD stops competing with the distribution tool and focuses on what it actually does (skill methodology + evaluation framework + hypothesis testing).</impact>
  </decision>

HTML viz uses canvas-based force-directed layout with node type coloring, filter buttons, text search, and click-to-inspect panel showing edges. `gad graph stats` reads existing graph.json without rebuilding.</resolution>
      <keywords>cli,graph,visualization,html,force-directed</keywords>
    </task>

  <decision id="gad-199">
    <title>Full native planning-graph implementation — supersedes gad-197 hybrid approach</title>
    <date>2026-04-15</date>
    <summary>Operator accepted the Graphify concept but chose full native Node implementation over vendoring the Python package. Supersedes gad-197 (which recommended hybrid: native extractor first, Graphify as opt-in trial). Rationale: the planning-graph use case is fully structural — walking typed XML files with known schemas — and does not need Graphify's multimodal extraction (vision, audio, PDF). A 500-line Node module (lib/graph-extractor.cjs) covers 100% of the planning-graph extraction, query, and visualization needs with zero new dependencies. Result: 827 nodes, 1129 edges from GAD's own planning files. Query engine handles natural-language-ish patterns and structured flags. Measured 53x token savings vs full snapshot, 589x vs raw XML read. The `gad try graphify` path remains available for operators who want multimodal extraction on arbitrary folders, but the framework's own graph pipeline is self-contained Node. Python dependency NOT accepted into the framework core.</summary>
    <impact>Task 42.2-45 (native extractor) marked done. New tasks 42.2-47 (gad query) and 42.2-48 (gad graph) shipped same session. .planning/graph.json and .planning/graph.html are new artifacts. gad-197 hybrid approach superseded — no Python in the framework dependency tree. The graph can be regenerated on demand via `gad graph build` and queried via `gad query`.</impact>
  </decision>

</decisions>

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: live planning data SSE stream + agent presence detection

/api/dev/live SSE endpoint watches .planning/ files (STATE, TASK-REGISTRY,
DECISIONS, ROADMAP) and pushes change events to the browser in real time.
Agent presence inferred from change pattern clustering — concurrent changes
to different files within 2s windows suggest multiple agents.

LiveDataPanel in editor right pane shows:
  - Current phase/milestone/task counts (auto-refreshing)
  - Detected agent count with last-seen timestamps
  - Recent file change log
  - Green/red connection indicator
  - Reconnect button on disconnect

Snapshot mode (?snapshot=1) returns one-shot JSON for non-streaming consumers.
Heartbeat every 15s keeps connection alive. Zero new dependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

git add \
  site/app/projects/edit/\[id\]/DnaActionRow.tsx \
  site/app/projects/edit/\[id\]/DnaEditor.tsx \
  site/app/projects/edit/\[id\]/ProjectEditor.tsx \
  lib/graph-extractor.cjs \
  .planning/gad-config.toml \
